thesis: The same orderbook that runs perps now runs spot. The router era is over for the markets that matter; there is a real book underneath.
through_action: to extend the orderbook onto spot
obstacle: Reader assumes 'spot on Infinex' still means swap-routing (swidge), or assumes this is another aggregator. The word 'spot' is cheap; the word 'CLOB' has to do work without being technical theatre.
reader_prior: I've been swidging spot tokens on Infinex. I trade perps on Hyperliquid through Infinex. I accept the routing tax on spot as the cost of staying in one place.
lining: surface: a calm notice that the book is open. underneath: the routing workaround is over for the markets that count; you can stop paying that tax.
not_the_point: Avoid '298 markets,' avoid TVL bragging, avoid framing this as competing with CEXes, avoid urgency vocabulary, avoid em-dash press-release rhythm.

x.physical_score: Steady declarative weight. The line plants the venue without effort spike.
x.obstacle_local: Reader scrolling past another 'live now' tweet.

x.physical_score: Even-tempo enumeration of tickers. Listed, not pitched.
x.micro_objective: to show depth without selling each line

web.physical_score: Single declarative surface. Weight without acceleration.
web.obstacle_local: Reader expects either a hero pitch or technical jargon; deliver neither.

in-product.physical_score: A compact UI label. Direct, light, finished.
in-product.obstacle_local: Sub-account model is new — clarity must beat cleverness.
